title.zh: 邊界之繩：為何世界邊界正處於崩潰邊緣？
title.en: The Fragile Tapestry: Why the World's Borders Are More Stable Than They Seem

summary.zh: 【總結】這篇研究揭開國際秩序的隱藏韌性：儘管俄烏戰爭、中國擴張與美國退縮引發恐慌，但全球邊界秩序並非如預期般脆弱。作者以「邊界如同織物」的隱喻，揭示80%的世界邊界已穩定超過20年，且新衝突在和平超過20年後幾乎消失。這項突破性發現顛覆「歷史重演」的危言聳聽，為全球治理提供關鍵基準。【研究問題】過去學界聚焦單一衝突事件（如俄烏戰爭），卻忽略邊界穩定的系統性機制。當前研究缺口在於：為何戰後國際秩序維持近80年？當權力重構、美國退縮與norm崩壞同時出現，邊界為何未全面崩潰？【研究方法】作者結合歷史數據（圖1顯示戰後邊界變動急劇下降）、案例分析（俄烏戰爭、中國南海主張）與制度機制研究。獨特之處在於將「邊界穩定」解構為「個別國對」與「區域均衡」雙層機制，並用「邊界年齡」作為預測衝突的關鍵指標。【結果】數據顯示：80%的國對邊界從未發生爭端或20年以上未有衝突；和平超過20年後，新衝突機率僅0.5%。俄烏戰爭雖震撼，但僅占全球邊界爭端的2%。拉丁美洲的「邊界協調規範」與非洲的「殖民邊界遺產」證明，區域均衡比單一國家強權更關鍵。
summary.en: **Summary:** This research unveils a hidden resilience in the global territorial order: despite alarms over Russia's invasion of Ukraine, China's assertiveness, and U.S. retreat, international borders are far more stable than feared. Using the metaphor of borders as a woven tapestry, the study reveals that 80% of world borders have remained peaceful for over 20 years, with new disputes becoming vanishingly rare after two decades of peace. This breakthrough challenges the narrative of a "return of conquest" and provides critical benchmarks for global governance. **Problem:** Previous scholarship focused on isolated conflicts (like Ukraine), ignoring systemic mechanisms that sustain border stability. The critical gap is: Why has the post-WWII order endured for 80 years? When power shifts, U.S. credibility wanes, and norms erode simultaneously, why haven't borders collapsed? **Approach:** The author combines historical data (Figure 1 shows a sharp decline in territorial changes post-WWII), case studies (Russia's Ukraine invasion, China's South China Sea claims), and institutional mechanisms research. A unique strength is decomposing border stability into two layers: dyadic (state-to-state) relationships and regional equilibria, with "border age" as a key predictive metric. **Results:** Data shows 80% of contiguous state pairs have never disputed borders or resolved disputes over 20 years ago; after 20 years of peace, new disputes occur in just 0.5% of cases. The Ukraine war, while dramatic, represents only 2% of global territorial disputes. Latin America's "border coordination norms" and Africa's colonial border legacy demonstrate that regional equilibrium matters more than individual great powers.

keyArticle.title: Holding the World Together? The Future of Territorial Order
